channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Switch from Ruby CSV to SmarterCSV in 5 Minutes
item.description: Why switch? → 10 Ways Ruby's CSV.read Can Silently Corrupt or Lose Your Data In this article we'll explore how easy it is to switch from Ruby CSV to SmarterCSV — often just a single line change....

item.title: 🚀 Day 19 of My Automation Journey – Method Overloading Tricky Questions
item.description: Today I practiced some tricky scenarios of Java Method Overloading. Method overloading is a very common concept in Java, but the way Java decides which method to call can sometimes be confusing....

item.title: Tracing a RAG Chain End-to-End: Where OpenTelemetry Stops and Where You Need to Instrument Yourself

item.title: Why <label> and <button> Are Better Than <div> for Forms

item.title: 10 Ways Ruby's CSV.read Can Silently Corrupt or Lose Your Data

item.title: Context Engineering Is Not a Replacement for Architecture
item.description: Context is a last-mile influence layer, not a defining layer. Architecture still governs behavior, constraints, and system physics. Context Engineering Is Not a Replacement for Architecture...

item.title: Yahoo CEO Jim Lanzone on reviving the web’s homepage

item.title: This chair gives half-worn clothes a home
item.description: Seating technology may have reached its peak for those of us who struggle to keep our lightly worn clothing piles away from furniture. The Laundry Chair - developed by YouTuber and viral inventor...

item.title: How to watch Jensen Huang’s Nvidia GTC 2026 keynote — and what to expect
item.description: GTC is Nvidia's flagship annual event, where the chipmaker typically announces new products, partnerships, and its vision for the future of computing. Huang's keynote will focus on Nvidia's role...

item.description: Some weeks in security feel normal. Then you read a few tabs and get that immediate “ah, great, we’re doing this now” feeling. This week has that energy. Fresh messes, old problems getting...

item.title: Nurturing agentic AI beyond the toddler stage

item.description: Last week, a few posts about a so-called virtual "embodied fly" tore through X, boosted by AI hype accounts and excited commenters who didn't seem to understand what it was they were excited...

item.title: The Download: glass chips and “AI-free” logos
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Future AI chips could be built on glass Human-made glass...

item.title: ClickFix Campaigns Spread MacSync macOS Infostealer via Fake AI Tool Installers
item.description: Three different ClickFix campaigns have been found to act as a delivery vector for the deployment of a macOS information stealer called MacSync. "Unlike traditional exploit-based attacks, this...

item.title: OpenAI’s adult mode will reportedly be smutty, not pornographic
item.description: OpenAI's delayed "adult mode" for ChatGPT is expected to support saucy text conversations at launch, but not the chatbot's ability to generate images, voice, or video. Speaking to The Wall Street...

item.title: Securing digital assets against future threats
My fitness tracker is a secret weapon against my chronic illness
item.description: One of the first major crashes I experienced as a chronically ill person happened on an unusually sunny January day in New York City. It was 2023, and I was riding my bike with a friend, flying...

item.title: Possible New Result in Quantum Factorization
item.description: I’m skeptical about—and not qualified to review—this new result in factorization with a quantum computer, but if it’s true it’s a theoretical improvement in the speed of factoring large numbers...

item.title: DRILLAPP Backdoor Targets Ukraine, Abuses Microsoft Edge Debugging for Stealth Espionage
item.description: Ukrainian entities have emerged as the target of a new campaign likely orchestrated by threat actors linked to Russia, according to a report from S2 Grupo's LAB52 threat intelligence team. The...

item.title: Models Are Applying to Be the Face of AI Scams
item.description: Dozens of Telegram channels reviewed by WIRED include job listings for “AI face models.” The (mostly) women who land these gigs are likely being used to dupe victims out of their money.
